Work Edtech Marketing The EdTech Mentor Resources About Let's Talk!
EdTech Marketing

Driving relevance within niche audiences.

Data-driven and insight-based campaigns work because they speak directly to the decision-makers who matter most. Through audience layering, messages align with specific needs and contexts, increasing relevance across the entire B2B EdTech buying journey.

Book a strategy session
The practice

The buyer is rarely one person.

A campaign aimed at a CIO reads differently than one aimed at a provost, a procurement lead, or a department head — even inside the same institution. This practice is built around that reality: layering audience data, account intelligence, and channel-specific execution so each message reaches the right decision-maker, in the right context, at the right stage of a long B2B EdTech buying journey.

ABM campaigns Outbound email sequences LinkedIn prospecting Account segmentation HubSpot orchestration Clay / Apollo data enrichment Event pre/post campaigns Demand generation playbooks
Clients

Reaching the decision-makers who matter.

Student First

A Student Information System challenger entering a market dominated by Ellucian and PowerSchool. We run a full demand-generation program engaging multiple buyer personas and C-level decision-makers across higher education.

Doctums

A management and technology consulting firm focused exclusively on education, led by former Ellucian and Oracle executives. Our engagement spans demand generation targeting higher-ed CIOs.

Ellucian

Higher education's leading SIS/ERP provider. Our work spans account-targeted campaign support across Ellucian's Latin America operations.

How a program runs

Built on audience layering.

Audience Layering

Every program starts with a curated, enriched contact list built from Ideal Customer Profile criteria: institution type, role, geography, technology stack. Built using Clay for enrichment, Apollo for database access, and LinkedIn Sales Navigator — then segmented in HubSpot before any outreach begins.

ABM Outbound Workflows

Structured, multi-step LinkedIn engagement sequences targeting specific accounts: visit profile, react to a recent post, connect, message — paired with a contact list, a HubSpot scheduling page, and a supporting email nurture sequence.

The Quarterly Operating Cycle

Weeks 1–2

Align & Prioritize — confirm themes, select format, lock the editorial calendar.

Weeks 3–6

Produce & Publish — first external asset live by week 4.

Weeks 7–10

Activate & Nurture — distribution, lifecycle drips, SEO monitoring.

Weeks 11–12

Learn & Tune — review performance, feed insight into next quarter.

Services in this practice

GTM Playbooks & Launch Campaigns

Marketing Operations & CRM
